prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1775468103224,"sessionID":"ses_29ddaffd4ffeUEbe5b5WqH658d","part":{"id":"prt_d62251190001Owbjj15tdorbTu","sessionID":"ses_29ddaffd4ffeUEbe5b5WqH658d","messageID":"msg_d6225051f0013e2GFvVC71MR82","type":"tool","callID":"chatcmpl-tool-97306f3f5802bf6f","tool":"write","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"content":"import argparse
import sys

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate a systemd service unit file.")
    
    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Name of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")
    
    # Optional arguments
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Description of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--after", default="network.target", help="Units that must start before this service (default: network.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart", 
                        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"], 
                        default="on-failure", 
                        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", default="5", help="Seconds to wait before restarting (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target", help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", help="Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="WorkingDirectory path")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    name = args.name
    description = args.description if args.description else f"{name} service"
    output_file = args.output if args.output else f"{name}.service"

    content = ["[Unit]"]
    content.append(f"Description={description}")
    content.append(f"After={args.after}")
    content.append("")
    
    content.append("[Service]")
    if args.user != "root":
        content.append(f"User={args.user}")
    content.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec}")
    
    if args.working_dir:
        content.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")
    
    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            content.append(f"Environment={env_var}")
            
    if args.restart != "no":
        content.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
        content.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    
    content.append("")
    content.append("[Install]")
    content.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")

    try:
        with open(output_file, "w") as f:
            f.write("\n".join(content) + "\n")
        print(f"Service file generated: {output_file}")
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error writing to file {output_file}: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

files.content:
import argparse
import sys


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate a systemd service unit file."
    )

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Name of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")

    # Optional arguments
    parser.add_argument(
        "--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as (default: root)"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Description of the service")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        default="network.target",
        help="Units that must start before this service (default: network.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        choices=[
            "no",
            "on-success",
            "on-failure",
            "on-abnormal",
            "on-watchdog",
            "on-abort",
            "always",
        ],
        default="on-failure",
        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        default="5",
        help="Seconds to wait before restarting (default: 5)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env", action="append", help="Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="WorkingDirectory path")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    name = args.name
    description = args.description if args.description else f"{name} service"
    output_file = args.output if args.output else f"{name}.service"

    content = ["[Unit]"]
    content.append(f"Description={description}")
    content.append(f"After={args.after}")
    content.append("")

    content.append("[Service]")
    if args.user != "root":
        content.append(f"User={args.user}")
    content.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec}")

    if args.working_dir:
        content.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")

    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            content.append(f"Environment={env_var}")

    if args.restart != "no":
        content.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
        content.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")

    content.append("")
    content.append("[Install]")
    content.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")

    try:
        with open(output_file, "w") as f:
            f.write("\n".join(content) + "\n")
        print(f"Service file generated: {output_file}")
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error writing to file {output_file}: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
